nativeRange.habitat: Steep outer reef slopes, typically in small tracts of rubble between areas of rich coral growth; reported from depths of about 20–115 m, where light levels are subdued

diet.notes: Has been documented grazing on microalgae and small crustaceans in the wild. In aquaria it is generally reported to feed readily; a varied diet is commonly advised, including spirulina, marine algae, a quality prepared marine angelfish formula, and enriched frozen mysis or other meaty items. Feeding small amounts 2–3 times daily tends to help discourage nipping at corals and clam mantles.

summary: A small, prized deepwater dwarf angelfish from the central and western Pacific, with pale peach to yellow body, blue markings, and black speckles. Generally semi-aggressive and best kept singly in well-established reefs with subdued lighting.
careNotes: The Multicolor Angelfish is a deepwater Pacific Centropyge recorded from roughly 20–115 m on steep outer reef slopes, which is reflected in its preference for subdued aquarium lighting and ample shaded rockwork. It is rarely exported and commands a premium in the trade. Sources generally describe it as reasonably hardy once acclimated and feeding readily, but it should be the last fish introduced to a peaceful community and given a mature aquarium with abundant live rock for grazing and shelter alongside open swimming space. Sources moderately disagree on the minimum tank size: LiveAquaria cites 70 US gal (~265 L), other vendors 55–75 gal (~208–284 L), and Reefapp 300 L; 300 L has been retained here as a cautious round figure consistent with this species' need for territory and grazing surface. Sources also disagree on temperature — LiveAquaria advises 22–26°C (72–78°F) for captive husbandry while FishBase reports a wild thermal preference of about 24–29°C; the lower aquarium range has been kept here as it aligns with related Centropyge husbandry. Reef compatibility is generally listed as 'with caution': it has been known to nip fleshy stony coral polyps, soft corals, and clam mantles, and frequent feeding tends to reduce this. The species is reported to be sensitive to copper-based medications. Handle with care, as angelfishes have sharp cheek (preopercular) spines that can tangle in nets.
breedingNotes: Like other dwarf angelfishes, Centropyge multicolor is generally reported as a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite that forms harems of about 3–7 individuals with one dominant male; if the male is lost, the dominant female commonly transitions to male. It is a dusk pelagic broadcast spawner with no parental care. Captive larval rearing has been reported only by specialist breeders and is considered very difficult at hobbyist level.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary source for husbandry parameters: 70 gal minimum, 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, SG 1.020–1.025, semi-aggressive, reef-safe with caution, omnivore.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, family/order, max size (9.0 cm TL), depth range (20–115 m), distribution across Micronesia/Fiji/Cook/Society Islands plus Tonga, harem formation (3–7 individuals), and wild thermal preference (24.3–28.9°C — warmer than the LiveAquaria aquarium range; aquarium-typical 22–26°C retained).

additional.name: Reefapp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (9 cm), minimum tank size (300 L — larger than LiveAquaria's 70 gal/265 L), reef caution ('reef safe with luck'), aggression toward conspecifics and similar species, and protogynous hermaphroditism.

fish.breedingNotes: Protogynous hermaphrodite and haremic dusk pelagic spawner; harems of about 3–7 fish with one dominant male. Captive larval rearing reported only by specialist breeders; not commercially bred at hobbyist level.
